top_findings.summary: This skill description instructs users to upload potentially copyrighted video frames to a third-party service (SlowPics) without any disclosure about data handling, retention, or privacy practices. While the stated purpose is comparing video quality, the workflow requires uploading user media content to an external server without clear necessity (local comparison tools like mpv or vs-preview could achieve similar results locally). The skill also implies accessing local filesystem paths (like `~/.ssh` via `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` references) which is excessive for video analysis. Additionally, there's no mention of obtaining user consent before uploading potentially sensitive media content to third-party servers.
